cards.back: A Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) data structure that follows the principle of addition and removal from the top
A First-In-First-Out (FIFO) data structure that follows the principle of addition to the end and removal from the front
A method that calls itself in its own definition, allowing a function to be repeated multiple times

cards.front: What is a tree data structure?
cards.back: A hierarchical data structure composed of nodes, where each node has a value and zero or more child nodes

cards.front: What is a graph data structure?
cards.back: A non-linear data structure composed of nodes (vertices) and edges that connect the nodes
